title: Pressure-test typed intake artifact flow
challengeContext: A domain-neutral harness should not treat adversarial intake notes as final planning conclusions.
challengedAssumptions: A raw request is sufficient for planning
One producer can define its own divergent schema
edgeCases: Malformed Work Brief carrier reaches plan intake
Producer source skill does not match the callable producer
acceptanceGaps: Need proof that raw chat is rejected by plan intake
riskCandidates: False green if the seam validates shape without actual artifact validator
background: Adversarial intake captures gaps before planning starts.
problemOrOpportunity: Pressure-testing can reveal missing Work Brief evidence before plan consumes it.
desiredOutcome: The grill-me producer writes a validator-accepted Work Brief through the common writer.
constraints: Use the shared Work Brief class
userVisibleBehavior: Users can challenge assumptions and still hand off a normal Work Brief
risksAndUnknowns: Candidate risk wording must remain planner input only
acceptanceNotes: Produced grill-me Work Brief validates through the artifacts package
Plan intake consumes the produced Work Brief as ready input
